chunk_summary: This section discusses ethical, equitable, and safe use of low-bandwidth communication channels and messaging platforms in Indian tele-nutrition, referencing key guidelines and data protection laws.
learning_objectives: Understand the ethical considerations in using low-bandwidth communication channels for tele-nutrition in India.
Learn about the Digital Personal Data Protection Act, 2023, and its implications for tele-nutrition practices.
Identify safe practices for using messaging platforms in tele-nutrition consultations.
Explore the role of WHO and WMA guidelines in shaping telemedicine ethics and equity.
key_facts: The Telemedicine Practice Guidelines (2020) provide a framework for safe and ethical telemedicine practices in India.
The Digital Personal Data Protection Act, 2023, emphasizes user consent and data security in digital health services.
Low-bandwidth communication channels are critical for equitable access in rural and underserved areas of India.
WHO guidelines recommend digital interventions to strengthen health systems, including tele-nutrition.
Messaging platforms must comply with data protection laws to ensure patient confidentiality and security.
reference_quote: "The Digital Personal Data Protection Act, 2023, emphasizes the importance of user consent and data security in digital health services." (Source: https://www.meity.gov.in/static/uploads/2024/06/2bf1f0e9f04e6fb4f8fef35e82c42aa5.pdf)
